text: Fructose-glucose composite carbohydrate beverages (0.5-1.0:1 ratio) enhance endurance performance by 1-9% in mean power compared to glucose-only formulations during 2.5-3.0 hour exercise.
A 0.8:1 fructose-to-glucose ratio ingested at 1.5-1.8 g/min provides the highest exogenous carbohydrate energy delivery and endurance power compared to lower or higher fructose-glucose ratios.
Fructose-glucose composites at higher ingestion rates (≥1.7 g/min) produce larger performance improvements (4-9%) than at moderate rates of 1.4-1.6 g/min (1-3%).
Fructose-glucose composite beverages reduce gastrointestinal distress compared to isocaloric glucose-only beverages at equivalent carbohydrate ingestion rates.
Solutions containing a 0.7-1.0:1 fructose-to-glucose ratio are absorbed fastest among carbohydrate composite formulations.

Performance benefits of fructose-glucose composites are associated with increased exogenous carbohydrate oxidation rate and improved unilateral fluid absorption compared to single-saccharide formulations.
The minimum carbohydrate ingestion rate for meaningful performance benefits from fructose-glucose composites is approximately 1.3 g/min, with effects established up to 2.4 g/min.
